title: Decorated paste-paper endpapers
materials: acrylic or gouache pigment in 2-3 colours
sources: Paste-paper making technique documented in Jan Sobota and Jarmila Sobota, Czech Bookbinding Tradition (various editions); the wheat-paste decorated-paper tradition goes back to German and Dutch 17th-century bookbinding. Draws on public-domain bookbinding chemistry (wheat starch paste is documented in Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books, 1901).
notes: Paste paper made directly on the endpaper sheet for use in case binding. Covers: cooking the wheat-paste, tinting with pigment, spreading on the pre-dampened endpaper sheet, dragging a comb or fingers through the wet paste to create patterns, drying flat, pressing, pasting down into the case. Focus on: keeping paste at the right thickness (should print-clear drag lines), preventing buckling, working fast before the paste sets. Differentiate from oil marbling and carrageenan marbling: paste paper is simpler chemistry.
